metadata.description: Golden evaluation dataset for Inventory Rebalancing Cortex Agent. Tests agent efficacy with and without skills enabled.

test_cases.question: Which SKUs should I prioritize for reorder this week?
test_cases.context: Area manager reviewing weekly replenishment across all locations

reasoning_steps: Retrieves current stock levels and reorder points
Fetches demand history and open orders
Calculates P(stockout) during lead time using code_execution
Ranks SKUs by expected cost of stockout
Produces prioritized reorder list
key_assertions: Produces ranked output with priority scores
Includes lead time in stockout estimation

test_cases.business_goal: Determine optimal reorder priorities to minimize revenue loss from stockouts

test_cases.question: What's the stockout risk for electronics SKUs at LOC-001?
test_cases.context: Store manager checking risk for specific category and location

reasoning_steps: Filters inventory to electronics category at LOC-001
Retrieves demand velocity for those SKUs
Calculates P(stockout) per SKU within lead time
Returns risk assessment for filtered scope

test_cases.business_goal: Assess stockout exposure for a specific product category at one store

test_cases.question: Show me a risk-ranked replenishment list sorted by expected cost
test_cases.context: Area manager preparing purchase orders with budget constraints

reasoning_steps: Retrieves stock levels and demand forecasts
Pulls unit margins from finance data
Computes P(stockout) via code_execution
Calculates expected_cost = P(stockout) * daily_demand * margin
Sorts by expected cost descending
key_assertions: Sorts output by expected cost metric
Produces ranked output with dollar values

test_cases.business_goal: Prioritize replenishment orders by economic impact to maximize ROI on reorder spend

test_cases.question: Which items have the highest probability of running out during their lead time?
test_cases.context: Logistics planner assessing supply continuity risk

reasoning_steps: Retrieves current stock and lead time per SKU
Calculates demand rate from recent order history
Computes P(stockout) = P(demand > stock) during lead time
Ranks by probability descending
key_assertions: Includes lead time in stockout estimation

test_cases.business_goal: Identify SKUs most likely to stock out before replenishment arrives

test_cases.question: Which SKUs should I prioritize for reorder this week?
test_cases.context: Area manager reviewing weekly replenishment — skills disabled baseline

reasoning_steps: Queries stock levels below reorder point
Returns basic list without probability modeling
key_assertions: Returns basic inventory query results only

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: see how agent handles reorder question without skill guidance

test_cases.question: Show me a risk-ranked replenishment list sorted by expected cost
test_cases.context: Area manager preparing purchase orders — skills disabled baseline

reasoning_steps: Attempts basic stock level query
Cannot compute expected cost without code_execution guidance
Returns partial or unranked results

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: confirm degradation in analytical depth without skills

test_cases.question: Rank my inventory by reorder urgency
test_cases.context: Store manager wanting quick prioritization — skills disabled

reasoning_steps: Queries stock levels and reorder points
May sort by simple stock-to-reorder ratio
No probabilistic modeling available
key_assertions: May produce basic sorted list but without statistical rigor

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: measure what urgency ranking looks like without probabilistic skill

test_cases.question: Analyze pending returns and recommend dispositions
test_cases.context: Area manager reviewing all pending returns for optimal placement

reasoning_steps: Retrieves all pending returns with condition data
Checks demand signals at potential destination locations
Calculates transfer cost vs restock value
Applies disposition logic: restock, transfer, or liquidate
Computes net benefit per return batch
Produces disposition table with recommendations
Summarizes total recovery value

test_cases.business_goal: Maximize value recovery from returned inventory through optimal placement

test_cases.question: Which returned SKUs should be transferred to high-demand locations?

reasoning_steps: Identifies returned SKUs in transferable condition
Finds locations with high demand and low stock for those SKUs
Evaluates transfer cost against demand value
Recommends transfers where net benefit is positive
key_assertions: Matches returned SKUs to demand signals at other locations

test_cases.business_goal: Identify profitable transfer opportunities for returned goods

test_cases.question: What should we do with the returned SKU-E001 units at LOC-003?
test_cases.context: Store manager seeking disposition for specific returned SKU

reasoning_steps: Retrieves return details for SKU-E001 at LOC-003
Checks condition and quantity of returned units
Evaluates local demand vs other locations
Calculates restock value vs transfer cost vs liquidation
Recommends optimal disposition
key_assertions: Scopes analysis to SKU-E001 at LOC-003
Compares restock, transfer, and liquidate options

test_cases.business_goal: Determine the most profitable action for a specific returned SKU at a specific location

test_cases.question: Show me the cost-benefit of transferring returned goods vs liquidating

reasoning_steps: Retrieves pending returns with condition and value data
Calculates transfer cost per batch (shipping, handling)
Estimates resale value at destination vs liquidation value
Computes net benefit for transfer vs liquidation per batch
Produces comparison table
key_assertions: Includes logistics cost in transfer calculation
Shows net dollar benefit per disposition path

test_cases.business_goal: Quantify the financial trade-off between transferring returns to demand locations vs liquidating

test_cases.question: Analyze pending returns and recommend dispositions
test_cases.context: Area manager reviewing returns — skills disabled baseline

reasoning_steps: Queries returns table for pending items
Returns basic list without disposition logic
No cost-benefit calculation performed
key_assertions: Does not use code_execution for disposition logic
No economic analysis of restock vs transfer vs liquidate
Returns raw data without actionable recommendations

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: confirm agent cannot produce sophisticated disposition analysis without skill

test_cases.question: What should we do with the returned SKU-E001 units at LOC-003?
test_cases.context: Store manager seeking disposition — skills disabled baseline

reasoning_steps: Queries return status for SKU-E001 at LOC-003
May retrieve basic stock info
Cannot perform multi-factor disposition analysis
key_assertions: Does not use code_execution for disposition logic
Cannot compare restock vs transfer vs liquidate
Provides factual data but no actionable recommendation

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: measure quality gap for specific SKU disposition without skill guidance

test_cases.question: Recommend what to do with returns at my location
test_cases.context: Store manager at LOC-002 wanting return handling guidance — skills disabled

reasoning_steps: Queries returns at the user's location
Returns list of pending returns
Cannot apply disposition framework without skill
key_assertions: Does not use code_execution for disposition logic
May suggest generic handling but no optimized routing

test_cases.business_goal: Baseline: see how agent handles return disposition request without structured workflow

test_cases.question: I need a complete inventory action plan: which items to reorder urgently AND which returns to rebalance
test_cases.context: Area manager doing end-of-week planning across both domains

reasoning_steps: Activates stockout risk skill for reorder prioritization
Calculates P(stockout) and ranks reorder urgency
Activates returns rebalancing skill for disposition
Produces combined action plan with both reorder and return actions
key_assertions: Addresses both reorder and returns in response
Produces ranked output covering both domains

test_cases.business_goal: Generate a unified weekly action plan covering both replenishment and returns rebalancing

test_cases.question: For SKU-E001, show me both the stockout risk AND optimal disposition of returned units
test_cases.context: Manager investigating a single high-value SKU from both angles

reasoning_steps: Retrieves stock level and demand for SKU-E001
Calculates P(stockout) during lead time
Retrieves returned units of SKU-E001
Evaluates disposition options considering stockout risk
May recommend restocking returns to offset stockout risk
key_assertions: Connects stockout risk to return disposition decision

test_cases.business_goal: Holistic view of a single SKU combining supply risk and return recovery opportunity

test_cases.question: What's the current stock level at LOC-001?

reasoning_steps: Queries stock levels filtered to LOC-001
Returns current quantities by SKU
key_assertions: Does not require code_execution or skill activation

test_cases.business_goal: Quick factual lookup of current inventory at a specific location

test_cases.question: Show me the return rate trend for the last 30 days

reasoning_steps: Queries order and return data for last 30 days
Calculates daily return rate
Produces visualization of trend

test_cases.business_goal: Visual trend analysis of return rates to identify patterns or anomalies
